_meta.description: Preferred English renderings for high-frequency Greek lemmas. Consult this glossary for consistency across translations. Update when introducing new preferred renderings.

θεός.note: Capitalize when referring to the God of Israel; lowercase for generic 'a god' in context.

κύριος.note: Can also mean 'sir' or 'master' in non-theological contexts.

Ἰησοῦς.note: Standard English form. Same Greek name as OT Joshua.

Χριστός.note: Literally 'Anointed One'. Use 'Christ' as standard; 'Messiah' in explicitly Jewish-context discussions.

πνεῦμα.note: Capitalize as 'Spirit' when referring to the Holy Spirit; lowercase otherwise (wind, breath, human spirit).

πίστις.note: Can also mean 'faithfulness' or 'trust' depending on context.

δικαιοσύνη.note: Some contexts warrant 'justice'. Note the connection to δίκαιος.

ἀγάπη.note: Distinct from φιλία (friendship-love) and ἔρως (romantic love).

λόγος.note: In John 1, capitalize as 'Word' (theological title). Otherwise 'word', 'message', 'reason'.

σάρξ.note: Can mean physical body, human nature, or sinful nature depending on context.

ἐκκλησία.note: Literally 'assembly' or 'congregation'. Use 'church' for NT ecclesial communities.

ἀπόστολος.note: Literally 'one sent'. Use 'apostle' for the Twelve and Paul; 'messenger' in generic contexts.

νόμος.note: Capitalize as 'Law' when referring to the Mosaic Law/Torah.

σωτηρία.note: Can also mean 'deliverance' or 'safety' in non-theological contexts.
